title: tanarius involucratus
intro: Tanarius involucratus, also known as Tanacetum balsamita, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Tanarius involucratus include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Tanarius involucratus include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
